title: neuropulse: a real-time 1:1 visualization of a full-scale LLM forward pass in the browser
description:
neuropulse renders a real forward pass of Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct in a web browser, 1:1 from live WebGPU activations — no server, no sampling, no approximation. The inference engine is a hand-written Phi-3 decode loop built from eleven WGSL compute kernels (add_norm, argmax, attention, attention_scores, embedding, fused_ffn, int4_matmul, int4_matmul_f32, kv_append, rms_norm, rope) over MLC's q4f16_1 ndarray-cache weights; each generated token is 292 GPU dispatches on the headless path and 348 with the visualizer active (the extra dispatches read back per-layer attention scores and eight logit-lens probes). Every intermediate tensor is read back and drawn in real time on a Three.js scene, so a 3.8-billion-parameter transformer can be watched as it runs.

The project is built as an empirical lab. A precision and reproducibility note (METHODS.md) documents the f16-weight / f32-accumulator budget, per-kernel ULP error bounds, and known divergences from a HuggingFace reference; a pinned cross-validation artifact (tools/reference/parity.json) carries weight URLs, SHA-256 hashes, validation prompts, and per-layer L2/cosine bounds at layers [0, 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 31], which the in-app validation suite re-derives and CI compares against the snapshot. A pre-registered ablation log (PREDICTIONS.md) files dated, falsifiable claims about which attention heads do what, with confirm/refute thresholds stated before each experiment runs.

Two research threads run on this platform. The neuropulse-native thread (experiment E45) tests a continuous-attention self-consistency fixed point against the standard single-pass forward and falsifies the zero-shot-inference variant of the hypothesis (a self-consistency cliff at iteration 2 that sharpens with model scale). A second thread — the Butterfly context-compaction falsification study — has been split into its own citable repository (github.com/abgnydn/butterfly); neuropulse retains the in-app Butterfly demo and the git-timestamped pre-registrations that seeded it. This deposit archives the visualizer, the inference engine, the methods and prediction logs, the E45 artifacts, and a manuscript draft (paper/neuropulse-manuscript.pdf).
notes: Original code, MIT-licensed. Weights are Microsoft's Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct redistributed by MLC in the q4f16_1 ndarray-cache format and are not part of this deposit. Generative AI was used as a coding and writing aid; all output was author-reviewed and validated against the pinned HuggingFace cross-validation artifact (tools/reference/parity.json) and the in-app validation suite.
